categories.title: Data Sources
categories.desc: APIs, feeds, and datasets for sports results, player stats, and tracking data. Coverage, freshness, and granularity vary across providers.

categories.title: Odds & Markets
categories.desc: Bookmaker APIs, exchange interfaces, and historical odds databases. Useful for real-time pricing, market access, and retrospective odds research.

categories.title: Models & Methods
categories.desc: Open source implementations of statistical models for match prediction and ratings. Includes Poisson, Dixon-Coles, Elo, and related frameworks.

categories.title: Edge Detection
categories.desc: Tools for measuring and tracking expected value, closing line value, and bookmaker margin. Useful for evaluating model output against market prices.

categories.title: Arbitrage & Scanning
categories.desc: Sure-bet and value-bet detection across multiple bookmakers. Includes commercial scanners with broad coverage and open source reference implementations.

categories.title: Prediction Markets
categories.desc: Platforms and APIs for event contracts on sports, politics, and other topics. Pricing data and historical resolution records are available for research.

categories.title: Python Libraries
categories.desc: Statistical and scientific Python packages used in sports analytics work. Covers distributions, Bayesian inference, time series, and general machine learning.

categories.title: Visualisation
categories.desc: Charting libraries, pitch plotting tools, and dashboarding frameworks for displaying model output and match data.

categories.title: FTWR Community
categories.desc: Repositories built under the FTWR organisation. MIT licensed. Open to contributions via pull request.

tools.name: football-data.org
tools.desc: Football results, fixtures, tables. Free tier covers 12+ leagues. No key required on lowest tier.

tools.name: API-Football
tools.desc: Comprehensive fixtures, live scores, stats, standings, and lineups. 100 free calls/day via api-sports.io.

tools.name: StatsBomb Open Data
tools.desc: Event-level tracking data for 30+ competitions. Includes Euros, WSL, La Liga. MIT licensed.

tools.name: tennis_atp (JeffSackmann)
tools.desc: ATP match-by-match results and rankings from 1968 to present. CSV files on GitHub.

tools.name: tennis_wta (JeffSackmann)
tools.desc: WTA equivalent of tennis_atp. Match results, rankings, and stats back to 1968.

tools.name: FBref.com
tools.desc: Football stats powered by StatsBomb data. Advanced metrics: xG, progressive passes, pressures, VAEP. No API -- scraping required.

tools.name: understat.com
tools.desc: Expected goals for 7 top European leagues going back to 2014/15. Shot-level xG with x,y coordinates. No API -- scraper available.

tools.name: basketball-reference.com
tools.desc: NBA, NCAA, G-League, WNBA historical stats. Box scores, play-by-play, advanced metrics, and splits. No API.

tools.name: OpenLigaDB
tools.desc: German football data. Bundesliga results and tables via free JSON API. No authentication required.

tools.name: Sofascore (unofficial API)
tools.desc: Live scores, lineups, player ratings, and detailed match events. Unofficial endpoints, not formally supported.

tools.name: courtspeed.com
tools.desc: Tennis court pace index (CPI) database. Surface-speed ratings per tournament and year, 2012 onward. Useful for adjusting match models to court conditions.

tools.name: tennis_slam_pointbypoint (JeffSackmann)
tools.desc: Point-by-point data for every rally across all Grand Slams since 2011. Includes shot sequences, serve details, and rally length. Well-suited for live models and Bayesian approaches.

tools.name: The Odds API
tools.desc: Real-time odds from 40+ bookmakers across 20+ sports. REST API with 500 free requests/month.

tools.name: Betfair Exchange API
tools.desc: Exchange API with full market depth, live prices, betting, and historical data. Documented REST and streaming endpoints.

tools.name: OddsPortal
tools.desc: Historical odds database across hundreds of leagues. Closing line data for model validation and CLV research. Scraping required.

tools.name: OddsJam
tools.desc: Live odds comparison across 50+ sportsbooks with a value-bet scanner and REST API access. US market focus.

tools.name: betfair-python (betfairlightweight)
tools.desc: Python client for the Betfair API. Covers streaming, betting, accounts, and navigation endpoints.

tools.name: penaltyblog
tools.desc: Python: Dixon-Coles, Poisson, Bivariate Poisson, Bayesian, and simulation. Covers most standard football prediction model approaches.

tools.name: tennis_match_prediction (jgollub1)
tools.desc: Python: Live in-match win probability model using Elo ratings combined with serve and return statistics. Updates continuously during a match as points are played.

tools.name: worldfootballR
tools.desc: R: Data wrapper for FBref, Understat, Transfermarkt, and Fotmob. Returns tidy data frames for analysis.

tools.name: SoccermaticsForPython
tools.desc: Code companion to Soccermatics. Pitch control, tracking data, Expected Threat, and network analysis. Learning resource and working implementations.

tools.name: statsbombpy
tools.desc: Python: Official StatsBomb data access library. Pull event data, freeze frames, and lineups directly into pandas DataFrames.

tools.name: ScraperFC
tools.desc: Python: Scrape FBref, Understat, Transfermarkt, Sofascore, and WhoScored. Minimal setup. Returns pandas DataFrames.

tools.name: understat (amosbastian)
tools.desc: Python: Async client for understat expected goals data. Fixtures, shots, player xG, and team-level statistics.

tools.name: openELO
tools.desc: Python: Elo, Glicko, and TrueSkill implementations for head-to-head sports rating. Parameterised for any two-player or team sport.

tools.name: pinnacle-api (community wrappers)
tools.desc: Python and Node.js wrappers for accessing Pinnacle pricing. Pinnacle closing lines are the market benchmark for CLV measurement.

tools.name: Kelly Criterion (ftwr-core)
tools.desc: Expected value and Kelly stake sizing implementations. Full fractional Kelly, simultaneous bets, and Kelly with uncertainty. Part of ftwr-core.

tools.name: Closing Line Value Framework
tools.desc: Methodology for measuring closing line value as a proxy for long-run profitability. Positive CLV is a common indicator that a model is pricing accurately.

tools.name: vig-free (margin removal)
tools.desc: Python: Remove bookmaker margin using Power, Shin, and multiplicative methods. Converts raw odds to true probabilities.

tools.name: rust-bet-tracker
tools.desc: Desktop bet tracker with 21 calculators, Kelly and fixed sizing, EV/CLV analytics, and per-book bankroll tracking. Local SQLite, no cloud sync. Rust + Tauri + Svelte.

tools.name: RebelBetting
tools.desc: Commercial value betting and arbitrage scanner. Tracks closing line value across 60+ bookmakers with per-bet CLV reporting.

tools.name: OddsMonkey
tools.desc: UK-focused matched betting and arbitrage platform. Odds matcher, each-way arbitrage, and casino offers. Free tier available.

tools.name: BetBurger
tools.desc: Real-time sure-bet and value-bet scanner across 100+ bookmakers. API access for automated monitoring and alert systems.

tools.name: Trademate Sports
tools.desc: Value betting platform with a focus on CLV tracking and bankroll management. Transparent ROI tracking across all placed bets.

tools.name: arbitrage-scanner (open source)
tools.desc: Python: Build-your-own arbitrage scanner using The Odds API. Reference implementation for cross-market sure-bet detection.

tools.name: Polymarket API
tools.desc: Crypto-based prediction markets on current events, sports, and elections. REST and WebSocket API with full market depth and order book access.

tools.name: Manifold Markets API
tools.desc: Free-to-play prediction markets. Full REST API with historical resolution data. Good for model calibration without real-money constraints.

tools.name: Metaculus API
tools.desc: Calibrated community forecasting with tracked resolution history. Long-range questions with publicly visible prediction distributions.

tools.name: Kalshi API
tools.desc: CFTC-regulated US event contracts. Sports, economics, weather, and policy. REST API with authenticated trading. Real money, legal in the US.

tools.name: py-polymarket
tools.desc: Python: Community client for Polymarket trading and data. CLOB API wrapper with order placement and market data streaming.

tools.name: scipy.stats
tools.desc: Distributions, statistical tests, continuous and discrete random variables. Includes Poisson, negative binomial, beta, and related distributions.

tools.name: PyMC
tools.desc: Probabilistic programming and Bayesian inference. Markov Chain Monte Carlo for parameter estimation and model uncertainty quantification.

tools.name: statsmodels
tools.desc: GLMs, time series, survival analysis, and diagnostics. Logistic regression and Poisson regression with proper statistical inference.

tools.name: lifelines
tools.desc: Survival analysis for Python. Kaplan-Meier, Cox proportional hazards. Useful for modeling win streaks, tenure, and event timing.

tools.name: scikit-learn
tools.desc: Machine learning in Python. Gradient boosting, neural networks, and model evaluation pipelines. Broad coverage of classification and regression methods.

tools.name: sktime
tools.desc: Time series classification, regression, and forecasting. Useful for form analysis, sequential match data, and regime detection.

tools.name: mplsoccer
tools.desc: Python: Football pitch plots using Matplotlib. StatsBomb event data integration built in. Handles pitch drawing, heatmaps, and arrow plots.

tools.name: kloppy
tools.desc: Python: Standardized tracking data processing. Reads Tracab, SkillCorner, StatsBomb, and more into a common format. Cross-provider analysis made possible.

tools.name: Plotly / Dash
tools.desc: Interactive Python charts deployable as web dashboards. Supports scatter, bar, line, and custom chart types with hover and filter controls.

tools.name: Matplotlib
tools.desc: Python scientific visualization library. Fine-grained control over chart layout and styling. Publication-quality static output.

tools.name: Grafana
tools.desc: Open source metrics dashboarding. Connect to PostgreSQL, TimescaleDB, or InfluxDB for live model output monitoring and performance tracking.

tools.name: ftwr-core
tools.desc: Python/TypeScript toolkit for sports data collection, odds comparison, and expected value calculation.

tools.name: ftwr-models
tools.desc: Statistical models implemented cleanly: Poisson, Elo, Bradley-Terry, Dixon-Coles. Theory documented alongside code. Sport-agnostic by design.

tools.name: ftwr-data-streams
tools.desc: Real-time data pipeline tools. Live odds streaming, match event feeds, and WebSocket connectors for time-critical analysis.

tools.name: ftwr-arbitrage
tools.desc: Cross-market scanning and arbitrage detection. Sure-bet identification, value-bet flagging, and real-time monitoring. MIT licensed.
